mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning by institutional investors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly UNRATE change shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising consumer credit balances indicate synchronized expansion in durable goods demand and household leverage, which historically precedes broad-based equity outperformance over the subsequent quarter as corporate revenue accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or periods of rising HOUST and falling RSAFS produce identical equity returns to rising HOUST and rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high industrial production growth coupled with stable inflation, supply-side capacity expansions successfully meet demand without triggering price pressures, allowing equity risk premiums to contract and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average while CPIAUCSL remains below 3.0% annualized, or the signal flips direction during high-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand that directly supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in quarters with GDP growth > 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.0%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical equilibrium threshold for corporate debt servicing, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains above 3.0%, or the monthly rate change shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong non-farm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate indicate a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations upgrade.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window, or periods of rising PAYEMS and rising UNRATE produce identical equity returns to the joint expansion signal.

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery phases where real GDP growth accelerates from contraction to expansion, equity markets price in the earnings trough, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as risk appetite rapidly normalizes.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth transitions from negative to positive, or the signal produces negative returns during early-cycle expansions.
